name: Sam Wooding
abstract: Samuel David Wooding (17 June 1895 – 1 August 1985) was an American jazz pianist, arranger and bandleader living and performing in Europe and the United States.

works.t: Porgy and Bess: Act I, Scene 1. Summertime

quotes.text: And then that's the choir that played with Kenny Spencer and we played the "Ballad of America", you know, that Paul Robeson used to do. Kenneth Spencer did it and then we had the evening in Carnegie Hall. And we had quite a job. That's the one when I walked into Rae. And her mother brought Rae to me at that time, you see.
quotes.caption: Sam Wooding recalls Carnegie Hall performance with Kenneth Spencer

about_quotes.speaker: Doc Cheatham
about_quotes.evidence: I joined Sam Wooding and went to Europe with that band... I recorded with Sam Wooding in Paris.
about_quotes.doc_title: Doc Cheatham interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, New York City, New York, July 28, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Doc Cheatham
about_quotes.evidence: I left Chick Webb and went right straight to Sam Wooding. ... Yes we were together all the time up until -- until after Sam Wooding

about_quotes.speaker: Monk Montgomery
about_quotes.evidence: Sam Wooding sent for me -- he was organizing a band... with Sam Wooding's band... I went into Sam Wooding's band

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Shoffner
about_quotes.evidence: Al Wynn was with Sam Wooding. Oh yes. You know. In Europe, yeah. And him and Sidvick sent the telegram over to tell me, he said, I want to take him John and Sam Wooding.

about_quotes.speaker: Leslie Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: I told him that I’d always wanted an article on Sam Wooding... eventually we did run a two part interview with Sam Wooding that ended up in one of Chip’s books.
about_quotes.doc_title: Leslie Johnson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Ruth Lion
about_quotes.evidence: he went skating and he heard instead of there being skating that day, there was Sam Wooding, the orchestra... So he went and listened to them.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ruth Lion interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 15, 1998
